blocks.text: Quillbrook Appraisal Partners LLP
Attention: Dana Gray, General Manager, Parrish-Gibbs
Re: Engagement for Access Easement Review
Dear Dana Gray:

This letter confirms that {{fact:f:E-2020-001.client}} ("Parrish-Gibbs") has engaged Quillbrook Appraisal Partners LLP to review the access easements affecting the parcels identified in our correspondence with your office. The purpose of the review is to establish what rights of access exist of record, whether the conditions on the ground conform to them, and what effect any deficiency has on value. Our conclusions will be limited to what the record and the properties themselves support.

The work will proceed in the order we always follow. We will first assemble and read the recorded instruments. We will then walk each affected parcel and compare what is granted on paper with what exists on the ground. Where market evidence is required, every comparable will be verified before it is used. Nothing will appear in our report that cannot be traced to a document or an inspection.

Joseph Fisher, Senior Appraiser, will lead the engagement and direct the field program. David Ellison, Staff Appraiser, will conduct the parcel inspections and maintain the inspection record. Marcus Burnett, Research Analyst, will handle the records work and the verification of transaction data. I will review the file at each stage and will sign the report.

In accordance with our practice, the commercial terms of this engagement, including our fee and the date on which the work will commence, are set out on the signature page of this letter and are executed there rather than recited in the body. Please review them before countersigning.

If the terms are acceptable, please countersign where indicated and return one executed copy to this office. We appreciate the confidence Parrish-Gibbs has placed in the firm and will conduct the review with the care your reliance on it requires.
Terms of Engagement
Agreed and accepted:
Memorandum
To: David Ellison; Marcus Burnett
From: Joseph Fisher, Senior Appraiser
Subject: Kickoff, Access Easement Review for Parrish-Gibbs

The firm has been engaged by {{fact:f:E-2020-001.client}} to review the access easements affecting the Parrish-Gibbs parcels in scope. Fieldwork commences {{fact:f:E-2020-001.start}}. The engagement letter is in the file; read it before you start. The objective is narrow: establish what access exists of record, confirm or contradict it on the ground, and report the effect on value. We do not opine past the evidence.
Workstreams

items: Records: Marcus Burnett pulls the recorded instruments and title references for each parcel in scope and logs what is verified against what is merely reported.
Field: David Ellison inspects each parcel with me, documents existing access conditions, and keeps the inspection record current as we go.
Reconciliation: I compare the record to the ground, resolve or flag discrepancies, and draft findings in parcel order.
Review: the file goes to partner review before anything leaves the office.

blocks.text: Next Steps

Dana Gray, General Manager at Parrish-Gibbs, is our contact for site access and records requests; route anything intended for her through me so the client hears one voice. Marcus, have the instrument list assembled before fieldwork begins. David, build your inspection checklist from the instruments, not from memory. Questions in writing, please. The file is the record.
Working Session Record: Access Easement Review
Undated field record per firm document procedure; the session date is carried in the file name. Session held by telephone conference. Prepared by Marcus Burnett, Research Analyst.
Attendees

items: Joseph Fisher, Senior Appraiser, Quillbrook Appraisal Partners LLP
David Ellison, Staff Appraiser, Quillbrook Appraisal Partners LLP
Marcus Burnett, Research Analyst, Quillbrook Appraisal Partners LLP

blocks.text: Discussion

Records status: the instrument pull for the engagement for {{fact:f:E-2020-001.client}} is complete. One recorded instrument describes an access route that does not match the condition David Ellison documented on the ground. The discrepancy is verified on the record side; on the field side it is reported, not yet verified. Flagged for reconciliation.

Field status: inspections are proceeding in parcel order. Dana Gray confirmed site access arrangements and will provide the property file her office holds. Joseph Fisher directed that no finding be drafted for the discrepant parcel until the ground condition is re-inspected and the instrument re-read against it.
Action Items

rows: Re-inspect the discrepant parcel and photograph existing access
Re-read the recorded instrument against the field record; log verified versus reported
Reconcile the discrepancy and determine whether it affects the valuation approach

blocks.text: Status Report: Access Easement Review
Prepared for {{fact:f:E-2020-001.client}}. Prepared by Joseph Fisher, Senior Appraiser, Quillbrook Appraisal Partners LLP.
Status Summary

The review is on schedule. Records work is complete, field inspections are complete, and reconciliation is underway. One discrepancy between a recorded instrument and the condition on the ground was identified, re-inspected, and resolved on the record; it is addressed in the findings. Nothing in the file changes the scope agreed in the engagement letter.
Accomplishments This Period

items: Completed field inspections of every parcel in scope; David Ellison's inspection record is complete and filed.
Completed verification of the recorded instruments; Marcus Burnett's records log distinguishes verified entries from reported ones throughout.
Re-inspected the parcel where the recorded access description did not match the ground condition and reconciled the instrument against the field record.
Began drafting findings in parcel order.

blocks.text: Upcoming Work

Drafting continues in parcel order. The draft then goes to partner review. The report will be written so that it can be filed and relied upon without edits, and the file will support every conclusion in it. We will notify Parrish-Gibbs before delivery if review raises any matter requiring client input.
Budget
Work performed to date is within the engagement fee of {{fact:f:E-2020-001.fee}}. No change to the fee is requested and none is anticipated.
Risks

Retrieval of certified copies from the recording office has been slower than planned. The copies are not required for the findings but are required for the final exhibits; if they do not arrive before drafting concludes, the report will note them as pending. No other risk is open on the file.
